title: MLflow ML Platform on AWS - Project Case Study

headings.text: Ai Project-1 — End-to-End ML PLATFORM (IaC) FULL ML MODEL Life Cycle Management with Focus on MLFlow Tracking Server
Study Material - ML Platform on AWS

paragraphs: (Production-Grade ML Infrastructure + CI/CD-ready MLFlow - ML Platform + Deployment, Monitoring, Analytics)
AWS-CDK + MLflow Tracking Server with S3 and RDS + Sagemaker Ai + Endpoint Management.
Use Cases: Designed for cross-industry application in FinTech, HealthTech, Retail and SaaS Ai products.
Comprehensive Project Overview
AI / MLOps / Cloud Platform Engineering
Cross-industry (FinTech, HealthTech, Retail, SaaS AI products)
Domain: Machine Learning Platforms / MLOps Infrastructure
AWS (Amazon Web Services)
Infrastructure as Code with AWS CDK
Core Technologies Used
What problem did this project solve?
Architectural Overview

Built a cloud-native ML platform where MLflow runs on ECS Fargate (with RDS + S3), and SageMaker training jobs log experiments and models to the remote MLflow server. Infrastructure is fully provisioned using AWS CDK.

MLOps Platform Engineering + Production ML Systems: Pipelines using SageMaker training jobs integrated with MLflow experiment tracking and model registry; model promotion and deployment via MLflow → SageMaker endpoints.
CI/CD, containerisation and orchestration tools: Docker, ECS Fargate, ECR, Infrastructure as Code with AWS CDK (CI/CD-ready infra layer).
Technical Proficiency Demonstrated
Technical challenges and business value delivered
Delivered a reusable, production-grade ML platform architecture that can be extended to real business ML pipelines (fraud, forecasting, personalization, recommender systems).
Published image: rajeshgarigala/mlflow-pyfunc:2.16.2
Estimated monthly cost: ~$230/month (us-east-1). Assumes on-demand pricing, ~730 hours/month, continuously running deployment. Does not include continuous SageMaker endpoint.
Single line: Full architecture demonstrates production depth; live version keeps recurring cost low and demo accessible.
Architecture to AWS CDK construct mapping
Key features and functionalities
Code, diagrams, study material
Infrastructure as Code implementation for provisioning the complete ML platform on AWS.

content: Ai Project-1 — End-to-End ML PLATFORM (IaC) FULL ML MODEL Life Cycle Management with Focus on MLFlow Tracking Server (Production-Grade ML Infrastructure + CI/CD-ready MLFlow - ML Platform + Deployment, Monitoring, Analytics) AWS-CDK + MLflow Tracking Server with S3 and RDS + Sagemaker Ai + Endpoint Management. Use Cases: Designed for cross-industry application in FinTech, HealthTech, Retail and SaaS Ai products. Business & Production Value Project Summary Comprehensive Project Overview Project Category AI / MLOps / Cloud Platform Engineering Industry/Domain Cross-industry (FinTech, HealthTech, Retail, SaaS AI products) Domain: Machine Learning Platforms / MLOps Infrastructure Cloud Platform AWS (Amazon Web Services) Infrastructure as Code with AWS CDK Key Technologies & Concepts Core Technologies Used Platform Keywords MLflow AWS CDK (IaC) ECS Fargate SageMaker Model Registry Experiment Tracking ECR S3 Artifacts RDS Metadata Store IAM VPC Secrets Manager CI/CD-ready ML Platform Production ML Infrastructure Problem & Objective What problem did this project solve? Problems Solved Teams lack a production-grade ML platform to reliably track experiments, manage models, and deploy models at scale Missing proper infrastructure, security, and reproducibility in ML workflows No centralized system for the full ML lifecycle management Primary Objectives Design and deploy a scalable ML platform on AWS that supports the full ML lifecycle Train, track, register, and deploy models using MLflow + SageMaker integration Provide infrastructure-as-code (IaC) deployment using AWS CDK for reproducibility Solution & Architecture Architectural Overview Solution Overview Built a cloud-native ML platform where MLflow runs on ECS Fargate (with RDS + S3), and SageMaker training jobs log experiments and models to the remote MLflow server. Infrastructure is fully provisioned using AWS CDK. MLOps Platform Engineering + Production ML Systems: Pipelines using SageMaker training jobs integrated with MLflow experiment tracking and model registry; model promotion and deployment via MLflow → SageMaker endpoints. CI/CD, containerisation and orchestration tools: Docker, ECS Fargate, ECR, Infrastructure as Code with AWS CDK (CI/CD-ready infra layer). 1 Infrastructure Layer (CDK) 2 VPC Networking 3 ECS Fargate (MLflow) 4 SageMaker Training 5 Model Deployment AWS-CDK (IaC) Reference Architecture: Cloud-native ML Platform Architecture (Infrastructure Layer: CDK → VPC → ECS Fargate → MLflow → RDS/S3) YAML / IaC Mapping: AWS CDK synthesizes CloudFormation templates for full reproducibility of the ML platform infrastructure MLFlow - SageMaker Ai ML Lifecycle Layer: SageMaker → MLflow Tracking → Model Registry → SageMaker Endpoint Monitoring & Logging: MLflow experiment metrics, CloudWatch logs for ECS tasks, centralized artifact and model version tracking, reproducible runs Skills & Technologies Used Technical Proficiency Demonstrated Primary Skills MLOps Architecture (Advanced) Cloud Platform Engineering (Advanced) ML Systems Design (Advanced) Secondary Tools / Frameworks MLflow SageMaker SDK Docker boto3 MySQL (for RDS) Programming Languages Python (Primary language for ML pipelines and CDK) AWS Cloud & DevOps Tools AWS CDK ECS Fargate SageMaker ECR S3 RDS IAM VPC Secrets Manager Challenges & Outcomes Technical challenges and business value delivered Key Technical Challenges Remote MLflow integration with SageMaker Custom MLflow containerization State management on stateless compute Secure secret handling Network/IAM wiring across services How They Were Resolved Private MLflow service behind ALB (Application Load Balancer) Custom Docker images in ECR (Elastic Container Registry) RDS/S3 externalized state management IAM roles for ECS/SageMaker service communication Secrets Manager for secure credential handling AWS CDK for consistent infrastructure provisioning Business & Production Value Outcome Delivered a reusable, production-grade ML platform architecture that can be extended to real business ML pipelines (fraud, forecasting, personalization, recommender systems). Full Reference Architecture Amazon SageMaker Notebook / Studio for experimentation MLflow Tracking Server for experiment logging MLflow Model Registry for model versioning and lifecycle management Amazon S3 for training data and model artifacts Amazon ECR for training and inference container images SageMaker Training Jobs and SageMaker Inference for training and deployments ECS Fargate + RDS + NLB for self-managed MLflow stack Containerization & Reproducibility Published image: rajeshgarigala/mlflow-pyfunc:2.16.2 Amazon ECR for AWS-native deployment Docker Hub for public visibility and portability Versioned serving environment and frozen dependencies Business Value Reduces ML deployment friction by standardizing the path from training to inference Improves reproducibility with experiment tracking and model versioning Supports multi-team ML workflows and clearer governance Shortens the AI-to-production lifecycle Demonstrates cost-aware cloud design (full vs. live showcase) Cost Analysis (Full Always-On) Estimated monthly cost: ~$230/month (us-east-1) . Assumes on-demand pricing, ~730 hours/month, continuously running deployment. Does not include continuous SageMaker endpoint. Selected Cost Breakdown ECS Fargate (MLflow): ~$144.16/month (4 vCPU, 8 GiB) NAT Gateway: ~$32.85/month Network Load Balancer: ~$16.43/month RDS compute & storage: ~$24.64/month Public IPv4 & Secrets Manager: ~$11.35/month Cost-Optimized Live Showcase S3 + CloudFront for static frontend SageMaker Serverless Inference for live predictions S3 for artifacts, ECR for inference image MLflow used on-demand for demos (not 24/7) Single line: Full architecture demonstrates production depth; live version keeps recurring cost low and demo accessible. Architecture & IaC Mapping Architecture to AWS CDK construct mapping Architecture Component AWS CDK / IaC Implementation MLflow Server ECS Fargate Service with custom Docker image Experiment Tracking Backend RDS MySQL instance for metadata storage Artifact Storage S3 bucket for model artifacts and experiment data Model Training SageMaker training jobs with MLflow integration Model Registry MLflow Model Registry on ECS with S3 backend Model Deployment SageMaker endpoints provisioned via MLflow Networking VPC with public/private subnets, security groups Access Control IAM roles and policies for least privilege access Secrets Management AWS Secrets Manager for database credentials Load Balancing Application Load Balancer (ALB) for MLflow service Container Registry ECR repositories for custom MLflow images CI/CD Integration AWS CDK for infrastructure as code deployment Monitoring CloudWatch logs and metrics for all services Platform Capabilities Key features and functionalities Experiment Tracking Centralized tracking of ML experiments Parameter and metric logging Artifact storage for models and datasets Reproducible experiment runs Comparison of different model versions Model Management Versioned model registry Model staging and promotion workflows Automatic model versioning Model lineage and provenance tracking Collaborative model development Model Deployment One-click deployment to SageMaker endpoints A/B testing capabilities Canary deployments Automatic scaling based on load Rollback to previous versions Security & Governance VPC isolation for MLflow server IAM role-based access control Secrets management for credentials Encryption at rest and in transit Audit logging for all operations Assets & References Code, diagrams, study material AWS CDK Code Infrastructure as Code implementation for provisioning the complete ML platform on AWS.
